preserveUserBrands: assets/brands/* except assets/brands/example/ (example is updatable, user brands are not)

featureFlags._doc: Runtime feature flags read at Electron startup. Do NOT rely on these for security-critical defaults — a stale version.json on an offline client stays at the shipped values. Security-critical changes must land as code pushes with a version bump.

notes: v1.20.0: MCP-direct sidecar — use Merlin's full tool surface from Claude Desktop (or any MCP client: Codex, Cline, Cursor) instead of being tied to the in-app chat. Three additive components, zero regressions to existing flows. (1) Local IPC endpoint inside the desktop app (app/mcp-ipc-endpoint.js) — Unix domain socket on macOS/Linux (mode 0o600), Windows named pipe under default-deny ACL. Newline-delimited JSON protocol, three methods only: ping / tools/list / tools/call. NO vault/get, NO config/read — the endpoint refuses anything else, so a compromised shim cannot exfiltrate keys. Auth via 32-hex token rotated on every desktop-app boot, written to <stateDir>/mcp-shim-token (mode 0o600), constant-time-compared at every dispatch (Hard-Won Security Rule 5). The same wrapped tool array the in-app SDK uses is dispatched here — single registry, two consumers, brand-check + idempotency + preview gate + concurrency slot + redaction all apply uniformly. (2) Stdio MCP shim (app/merlin-mcp-shim.js) — ~400 lines, asar-unpacked so Claude Desktop can spawn it directly with the bundled Node binary. JSON-RPC 2.0 on stdin/stdout. Active-brand auto-injection: if the call is missing brand AND the renderer's <appRoot>/.merlin-state.json has activeBrand set, the shim injects it before forwarding. Mirrors the in-app [ACTIVE_BRAND] tag flow. Graceful degradation: if the desktop app isn't running, tools/list returns a single placeholder tool whose call surfaces a friendly 'open Merlin and try again' message instead of breaking the connection. (3) Claude Desktop autoconfig (app/claude-desktop-config.js) — atomic merge of {mcpServers: {merlin: {command, args}}} into the user's claude_desktop_config.json. Preserves all other mcpServers entries (Cline, Cursor, etc.) untouched. One-time prompt at first launch with three buttons: Add now / Skip / Don't ask again. Decision persisted to <stateDir>/.mcp-claude-desktop-prompt; 'never' suppresses forever, 'skipped' re-prompts only on a major version bump. Refuses to clobber a corrupt config file. Renderer-driven 'Add to Claude Desktop' button wired via mcp-autoconfig-add IPC; sidecar-status query via mcp-autoconfig-status. SAFETY: this is purely additive — the in-app SDK chat is unchanged, the in-process MCP server still uses the same tool array, no flow that worked in v1.19.4 has changed. Boundary preservation verified: vault stays in Electron, never touches the shim or Claude Desktop process; only tool inputs and redacted outputs cross IPC. Tests: app/mcp-ipc-endpoint.test.js (auth-token rejection, method allowlist, tools/list shape, tools/call routing, ping, malformed line, brand auto-injection — 12 cases) + app/merlin-mcp-shim.test.js (stdin parse, MCP envelope formatting, active-brand injection, handshake reading, placeholder fallback — 14 cases). | v1.19.4: HOTFIX — Meta manual-token modal: pasting an access token via right-click → 'Use my API key' on a brand-scoped tile (e.g. POG) silently failed with 'metaAccessToken required — paste your token and run this again', even though the token was correctly persisted to the brand vault. Root cause: ipcMain.handle('discover-meta-ids') in app/main.js spawned the Go binary with --config <global-config> only — the binary's loadConfig does NOT auto-merge .merlin-config-<brand>.json from disk, so the @@VAULT:metaAccessToken@@ placeholder (which the renderer wrote to the brand config file) never made it into the binary's Config struct, applyBrandVault had nothing to resolve, and cfg.MetaAccessToken stayed empty. Dashboard refresh works because it builds a strict brand-merged config in a temp file via buildStrictBrandConfig and passes THAT as --config (line ~6253). discoverMetaIds now mirrors the dashboard pattern exactly — temp config, brand-merged, deleted on completion (no decrypted credentials left on disk). REGRESSION GUARD comment added at the fix site documenting the live incident (brand=pog, 2026-04-28, screenshot in chat). v1.19.2: TWO landed-together features — (A) window-level paste/drop into the chat composer (replaces the v1.19.1 bulk-upload tile), and (B) OAuth client_id bootstrap from BFF (worker secret = single source of truth). | (A) Bulk-upload UX refactor — kill the tile, embrace window-level paste/drop. The dedicated 'Bulk upload' Magic-panel tile (shipped in v1.19.1) is retired. Modern apps (Linear, Slack, ChatGPT desktop, Claude.ai, Cursor) all handle file input as window-level paste/drop into the composer; the LLM sees attachments alongside the user's typed context and decides routing — much smarter than a rigid filename Jaro-Winkler matcher running on every drop. The IPC backend stays shipped, refactored into a reusable helper that's exposed to the new mcp__merlin__bulk_upload tool — the LLM calls it on demand when batch routing makes sense. | (B) OAuth client_id bootstrap — every provider's clientID now flows from the BFF (Cloudflare Worker secret <FOO>_CLIENT_ID) through the exchange response into cfg.OAuthFoo.ClientID. Hardcoded values inside oauth.go's getFooOAuth factories become first-launch fallbacks only; the canonical value lives in one place — the Cloudflare secret. Closes the LinkedIn debug-loop class of incidents. New CLAUDE.md Hard-Won Security Rule 17 documents the four required call sites for adding a new OAuth provider.
whatsNew: New: use Merlin from Claude Desktop. The tile-driven setup (brand picker, OAuth, daemons) stays in Merlin; the chat happens wherever you want — Claude Desktop, Codex, Cline, Cursor, any MCP client. On first launch (and after major upgrades), Merlin offers a one-click 'Add to Claude Desktop' that atomically merges itself into claude_desktop_config.json without touching your other MCP servers. Use it as a polished sidecar until the in-app chat catches up.

Security: your API keys never leave Merlin's encrypted vault. Claude Desktop only sees tool inputs and outputs — exactly the same boundary the in-app chat respects. The local IPC endpoint refuses any call outside ping / tools/list / tools/call, so a compromised MCP client can only invoke Merlin tools, never read your tokens.

Active brand syncs automatically. Pick the brand in Merlin's Magic panel; Claude Desktop tools pick it up without you having to type 'brand: pog' on every call. Override is still respected — explicit brand args are never overwritten.
Auth token rotates on every Merlin restart. A stale Claude Desktop tab from a previous session safely refuses old credentials and reconnects on the next call.

Fixed: any fal.ai model under any namespace now works — pass openai/gpt-image-2/edit, black-forest-labs/flux-pro, or any future-lab/model/v1 and Merlin sends it through unchanged. Previously the binary auto-prepended fal-ai/ to every slash-containing slug, corrupting non-fal-ai/ namespaces into 404s. Live incident: openai/gpt-image-2/edit was unreachable.

New: Cancel button on the chat status row. When a tool call hangs (slow website fetch, third-party API stall), a Cancel button fades in next to the spinner after 30s — one click stops the turn. Previously the only escape was force-quitting Merlin.

Fixed: 'go to my site and check products' / 'rescrape <url>' now reliably routes to the brand-scrape tool (which has hardened 5/15/90s timeouts) instead of the built-in WebFetch (which has no timeout). Live incident: trypog.co rescrape hung the chat for 5+ minutes.

Fixed: when Merlin can't find a Meta access token, the error now says exactly what to do — 'restart Merlin and retry' for brand-scoped tiles, or 'connect Meta via the Magic panel' for global. Previously the same five fatal sites all said 'paste your token and run this again' — which loops the user into the same failure when the actual bug was upstream.

Fixed: pasting a Meta access token via the right-click → 'Use my API key' modal on a brand-scoped tile now works end-to-end. Previously the discover step failed with 'metaAccessToken required' even though the token was correctly saved — the binary spawn passed only the global config, missing the brand's vault placeholder. Now uses the same brand-merged temp config the dashboard uses.

Paste screenshots or drag-drop files anywhere in the window — they attach to your next message as chips above the composer and Merlin decides what to do with them (Read the image, file it with a product, embed in an ad brief, whatever the context calls for).

Removed the dedicated Bulk upload tile from the Magic panel — the LLM is smarter about routing than a rigid filename matcher. The Go Jaro-Winkler matcher stays shipped, exposed as the new mcp__merlin__bulk_upload tool that Merlin calls on demand when batch-filing 5+ files into product folders is clearly the right move.

Hardened (paste/drop chip flow): pasted clipboard images materialize to assets/brands/<brand>/inbox/ with a SHA-prefixed filename that contains zero user-controlled bytes (eliminates injection); 25 MB cap on pasted blobs (clipboard is RAM); MIME allowlist enforced server-side; counter-based dragenter/dragleave prevents the flicker that single-boolean overlays produce when the cursor crosses child elements.

Hardened: every OAuth provider's client_id now flows from the Cloudflare Worker secret as the single source of truth. The binary's hardcoded values are first-launch fallbacks only — the canonical value lives in one place, never two. Closes a class of debug loops where 'is the binary's hardcoded ID the same as the secret?' couldn't be answered (Wrangler doesn't reveal secret values).

Hardened: 35 new Go tests (oauth_bootstrap_test.go) source-grep both the binary and worker.js to fail CI on any drift between the hardcoded fallbacks. Add a new OAuth provider in 4 places? CI checks all 4. Miss one? Test fails before merge.
